subject: Chemistry
title: Mustard Gas to Chemotherapy
description: A micro-lesson that makes Mustard Gas to Chemotherapy usable.

hook.text: The first cancer drug came from mustard gas—a World War I chemical weapon. Doctors noticed it destroyed white blood cells.

buildup.text: In 1943, an Allied ship carrying mustard gas was bombed. Survivors showed dramatic white blood cell drops.

discovery.text: Nitrogen mustard was tested on lymphoma patients. Tumors shrank dramatically—the first proof that chemicals could fight cancer.

twist.text: Mustard agents work by cross-linking DNA strands, stopping cancer cells from dividing. But they also harm healthy dividing cells.

climax.text: This side-effect problem defines chemotherapy: kill cancer fast while damaging the body as little as possible.

punchline.text: A chemical weapon repurposed to fight cancer. Destruction turned to medicine.

quiz.question: How was mustard gas discovered to have anti-cancer properties?
options: Soldiers exposed to it showed dramatic drops in white blood cells, suggesting it kills dividing cells
A chemist accidentally spilled it on tumor tissue in a lab
German scientists tested it on prisoners during World War II
